goal: Become test-ready to drive within ~5 months (20 weeks) with 2-4 practice sessions per week.
constraints: Local licensing rules may restrict automatic-only licenses in some jurisdictions.
Work schedule limits practice to weekends and a couple weeknights.
Minimum of 50 supervised hours logged before full license eligibility.
decisions: Evaluate and decide on transmission type strategy (manual vs automatic) to align with licensing rules and long-term driving goals.
Adopt manual transmission as the primary learning approach due to family car; assess licensing constraints accordingly.
entities: Phase 1 plan: Foundations Weeks 1–4
Phase 2 plan: Quiet-road integration Weeks 5–8
Phase 3 plan: Control to complexity Weeks 9–12
Phase 4 plan: Independent & varied conditions Weeks 13–16
Phase 5 plan: Consolidation & test prep Weeks 17–20
End-of-phase checkpoint examples (per phase)
Weekly practice minimums by phase
Mock tests every 6–8 weeks
Adaptation guidance for busy schedules
Weekly schedule adaptation for limited availability (weekends + weeknights)
Top priorities drills and targets with measurable outcomes
Session templates: Weeknight 30–45 minutes and Weekend 75–90 minutes
Measurable weekly checkpoints for progress
Extra efficiency tips for practice and study
Manual-first transmission emphasis from Week 1 due to family car

Gear engagement & hill-start quick progression for short sessions: Stage 1 static biting point; Stage 2 creep and move-offs; Stage 3 1↔2 gear changes; Stage 4 up/downshifts; Hill starts Stage A bite with handbrake; Stage B controlled rollout; Stage C foot-brake release; Stage D real-world hill practice; Short-session structure for weekdays and weekends.
Hours tracking plan to meet 50-hour requirement for license eligibility
Two-axis structure (activity + environment) for logging hours to ensure variety
Overlapping sessions to cover multiple activity/environment targets
Practical session plan examples for logging (log templates and examples)
Logging templates: date, duration, supervisor, activity tags, environment tags, maneuvers, supervisor initials, notes
Mock exam cadence and environment-specific practice integration
Environment exposure rotation strategy (rotate quiet/moderate/fast routes)
Test-center pattern integration: practice high-speed joins from slip roads and compact city parking within the same session to simulate test flow.
High-speed merge drills progression (without motorway)
Tight-park/compact parking drills using cones, parked cars, and time-pressure practice
Safety and supervision guidelines for higher-speed practice
Reference points and landmark cues for parking alignment
Micro target framework: 5 accelerations to target speed + 3 merges per session
open: Anxiety when approaching fast roads; stall occurred on two attempts requiring a long breather.
